Chordata > Actinopterygii > Gobiiformes > Gobioidei > Gobiidae

Brown shore goby

Drombus triangularis*

Updated Sep 2020

Where seen?

This little brown goby is often seen on many of our shores from sandy to silty shores near reefs, also among seagrasses and mangroves. Sometimes, in a group of several individuals of different sizes.

Features

Up to about 5cm, those seen about 3-5cm. It has a chubby face with small eyes and a camouflaging pattern of a dark head and body with irregular pale spots and blotches that break up the body outline.

Sometimes in a group of individuals of different sizes. Punggol, Jun 12
Pasir Ris Park, Apr 10

Living with a shrimp: This fish is sometimes seen sharing a burrow with a Smooth snapping shrimp . More about the goby and shrimp partnership .

Punggol, Nov 13 Photo shared by Marcus Ng on flickr .
Pasir Ris Park, Jun 08
Changi, Jun 04

**Species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.

On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Brown shore gobies on Singapore shores

On wildsingapore flickr

  • Photos for free download
  • Map of sightings in Singapore

Tanah Merah, May 09 Photo shared by James Koh on flickr .

References
